target_game.name: Archery Clash!
target_game.core_loop: Engage in turn-based weapon duels, earn rewards, and collect/upgrade gear to progress through increasingly difficult levels.
target_game.audience_proxy: Young adult males interested in competitive PvP, weapon upgrades, and stylized female characters.
target_game.visual_style: 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting.

palette.description: Bright blue banners with vibrant yellow text and colorful item rarity backgrounds.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · in-game

centroid_hook.visual_action: Blocks are placed quickly into a grid, filling it up while a progress bar moves from 'SMART' to 'GENIUS'.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 2d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Rapid block placement clearing a green grid."

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Revealing secret underground bunkers beneath famous landmarks like the Eiffel Tower.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A cross-section view pans down from the Eiffel Tower to show a complex multi-story underground shelter.

top_archetypes.rationale: 3 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 238d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Revealing secret underground bunkers beneath famous landmarks like the Eiffel To"

game_fit_scores.rationale: This is a block-puzzle "SMART/GENIUS/PSYCHOPATH" satisfaction hook — the visual language (grid filling, green palette, IQ-tier text overlay) belongs to casual puzzle/woodoku-style games, not a 3D PvP archery duel. Frictions: no aiming/trajectory, no character, no weapon fantasy, and the audience it pulls (casual puzzle players, often older/female-skewing) is misaligned with Archery Clash's young-male competitive PvP target. Skip this archetype unless you're testing a deliberate broad-audience puzzle-bait hook with a hard pivot to archery gameplay in seconds 3-5, and even then expect poor post-install retention.

This is essentially a licensed-IP static ad for Dokkan Battle (Goku Ultra Instinct) — the entire hook depends on anime brand recognition we don't have, so it's not a replicable archetype for Archery Clash without IP infringement risk. Mechanically there's zero overlap: no aiming, dueling, or gear progression is communicated, just a charging energy blast. Audience skews male/action-oriented which mildly overlaps, but the anime-collector RPG demo converts poorly into PvP archery; do not pursue this archetype.

This is a licensed Dragon Ball Z anime hook (Goku SSJ3 Dragon Fist) — using it for Archery Clash! would be IP infringement and is a hard no for paid UA. Beyond the legal block, the mechanic mismatch is severe: a melee energy-punch finisher communicates nothing about ranged aiming, trajectory arcs, or PvP duels, and the dark indigo/purple palette clashes with the game's bright blue/yellow brand. The only partial overlap is young-male power-fantasy audience, but you'd capture them far more cheaply with an original "satisfying one-shot kill" archery animation that borrows the beat (charge → release → spectacular finisher) without the IP.

This is a classic casual puzzle/sort hook (bus jam, stylus-driven, "relaxation" pitch) that targets a broad casual — typically older/female-skewing — audience seeking calm gameplay, which directly conflicts with Archery Clash!'s competitive PvP dueling loop and young-adult-male power-fantasy positioning. The mechanic mismatch is the dealbreaker: there's no path from a top-down bus-sort puzzle to aiming/shooting archery duels without misleading creative (high IPM but terrible D1/ROAS risk). Skip unless the team explicitly wants a fake-ad puzzle bait test, in which case at minimum reshoot with an archery-themed sort/clear puzzle and align palette to the game's blue/gold rather than the purple/orange member palette.

This archetype is a fundamental mismatch: the muted earth-tone palette (#2c3e50/#d35400/#2d5a27) and architectural cross-section animation style clash with Archery Clash!'s bright blue/gold stylized 3D PvP aesthetic, and there's no thread connecting "secret bunkers under landmarks" to aiming, dueling, or gear progression. Audience overlap is weak — curiosity/edutainment viewers skew broader and more passive than the competitive young-male PvP target. Recommend skipping unless we can radically reskin the hook (e.g., "secret archery training bunker beneath the Eiffel Tower" with a female protagonist reveal), but even then it's a speculative test, not a priority bet.

brief.title: SMART → GENIUS → PSYCHOPATH Trick Shots
brief.hook_3s: Arrow splits an apple mid-air — meter jumps from SMART to GENIUS in a blue-and-gold duel arena.
scene_flow: 0-3s: POV pull-back on bowstring in Archery Clash arena, arrow drills a small target — meter pings 'SMART' in yellow.
3-7s: Quick-cut second shot ricochets off a shield and pierces opponent's helmet — meter climbs to 'GENIUS', confetti pop.
7-11s: Third shot — drawn-back charged arrow splits an incoming arrow mid-flight then headshots the rival duelist — meter maxes 'PSYCHOPATH' with screen shake.
11-15s: Victory chest bursts open showing rarity-colored gear upgrades, female archer flexes bow, CTA card slams in.
brief.visual_direction: Authentic Archery Clash! 3D stylized look — slightly cartoonish female archer, vibrant colors, realistic lighting. Bright blue (#2A9DF4) arena banners and HUD chrome, vibrant yellow (#FFD700) trajectory arc and meter text, green (#32CD32) accent on the rising tier bar. Keep the existing aiming reticle, dotted trajectory line, health bars, and gear-rarity backgrounds visible in every beat — this must read as in-game footage, not a stylized cinematic.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Archery Clash! rotation leans on generic playables and a self-aware 'I paid for this ad' YouTube spot — they have zero satisfaction-tier escalation creatives. This brief imports the trending SMART/GENIUS/PSYCHOPATH meter format (high scroll-stopping power on TikTok/Reels) but hard-pivots it to authentic archery trick-shot gameplay in the first 3 seconds, so it inherits the meme's CTR without the puzzle-bait audience mismatch. Test against the Applovin playables as a video-source funnel.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic in-game screenshot of Archery Clash! mobile duel: stylized female archer at bottom of frame drawing back a glowing bow, dotted yellow (#FFD700) trajectory arc curving toward a rival duelist across a sunlit blue (#2A9DF4) arena platform, opponent health bar and gear rarity icons in HUD chrome, green (#32CD32) tier meter on right side filling toward 'GENIUS', 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting, on-screen text 'GENIUS' in bold yellow caps. [Audio: VO whisper 'wait for it…' / punchy upbeat trap 120BPM building tension / bowstring creak, arrow whoosh at 3s, ascending chime ping on meter tick]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, in-game Archery Clash! moment: a charged arrow mid-air splitting an incoming enemy arrow in two, sparks and motion lines, blue (#2A9DF4) arena banners blurred behind, yellow (#FFD700) impact burst, green (#32CD32) 'PSYCHOPATH' meter maxed top-right, aiming reticle still visible, 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting, on-screen text 'PSYCHOPATH 🎯' slamming center. [Audio: VO satisfied 'no way' / hard trap drop 140BPM / arrow-on-arrow metal clink, bass-boom impact, crowd-roar swell]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Archery Clash! victory screen: opened loot chest spilling rarity-colored weapon cards (blue #2A9DF4, gold #FFD700, green #32CD32 backgrounds), female archer character idle-flexing her bow on left, level progression bar filling, install button glowing yellow at bottom, 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting, on-screen text 'CAN YOU HIT IT?' in bold yellow caps. [Audio: VO confident 'your turn' / triumphant trap outro 128BPM / chest unlock chime, coin cascade SFX, UI tap on CTA]

brief.title: Bow Drawn — Static Hero Smash Cut
brief.hook_3s: Frozen frame: arrow nocked, bowstring trembling, blue energy crackling on the arrowhead — "TAP TO RELEASE."
scene_flow: 0-3s: Static hero frame — Archery Clash! female protagonist mid-draw, arrow glowing electric blue, frozen like a poster with giant 'PLAY NOW!' lockup.
3-7s: Frame unfreezes — aiming trajectory arc snaps across the dueling arena, opponent's health bar visible across the gap.
7-11s: Arrow fires, slow-mo impact, opponent HP shatters; rarity-colored gear drops with golden burst VFX.
11-15s: Quick cut to upgrade screen — bow tier glows up from blue to gold — end card with logo and CTA.
brief.visual_direction: Lean into the static-poster archetype but rebuilt around Archery Clash!'s 3D stylized look: slightly cartoonish female archer rendered with realistic rim lighting, bright #2A9DF4 banner backdrop, #FFD700 burst rays radiating from the drawn arrow, #32CD32 rarity glow on the dropped loot. Keep the in-game HUD (health bars, aim trajectory dotted line, gear slots) authentically visible — every frame should read like a real match screenshot, not a marketing render.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Archery Clash! rotation is almost entirely unmarked playables and a self-aware "I paid for this ad" video — they have zero static-hero/poster-style hooks despite that archetype trending in the scan. This brief ports the high-impact frozen-hero + giant CTA formula (minus any IP risk) onto authentic Archery Clash! gameplay chrome, giving UA a thumb-stopping top-of-funnel asset that complements rather than duplicates the playable inventory.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical mobile ad still: Archery Clash! female protagonist in mid-draw pose, bowstring fully tensioned, arrow tip crackling with electric blue #2A9DF4 energy, frozen poster-style composition. Authentic in-game HUD visible — opponent health bar at top, dotted aiming trajectory arc, gear slot icons bottom corner, turn-based duel arena floor. Vibrant #2A9DF4 banner backdrop with #FFD700 burst rays radiating from the arrowhead, #32CD32 rarity glow accents. 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting. Bold yellow on-screen text overlay: 'PLAY NOW!'. Must match IP-Adapter Archery Clash! reference exactly — same character proportions, same UI chrome, same dueling stage. [Audio: VO 'One shot. One duel. Play now.' / punchy competitive 128bpm trap with brass stab / bowstring creak at 0.5s, electric crackle hum, deep boom on CTA pop-in at 2.8s]

Storyboard frame 2, 9:16 vertical: arrow mid-flight slow-motion across Archery Clash! duel arena, motion-blur trail in #2A9DF4, opponent archer flinching as health bar cracks in #FFD700 numbers, #32CD32 rarity loot crate already glowing in foreground. In-game HUD intact — turn indicator, ammo count, character portrait. 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting. On-screen text: 'ONE SHOT. ONE DUEL.' in bold yellow. Authentic Archery Clash! gameplay still, not a render. [Audio: no VO / same 128bpm competitive trap drop / arrow whoosh 0-1s, heavy thud impact 1.5s, glass-shatter HP break 2s, coin chime on loot 3.5s]

Storyboard frame 3, 9:16 vertical: Archery Clash! upgrade screen close-up, bow weapon card tier-upgrading from blue #2A9DF4 common to gold #FFD700 legendary, +stat numbers popping in #32CD32, sparkle particles, authentic upgrade UI panel with star rating and rarity border. 3D stylized with vibrant colors, slightly cartoonish character models, and realistic lighting. Bold on-screen text: 'LOOT THE WIN'. Frame must read as the real Archery Clash! upgrade menu, identical UI chrome to reference screenshots. [Audio: VO 'Upgrade. Dominate.' / triumphant trap riser resolving on tonic / level-up chime stack at 1s, golden sparkle shimmer, satisfying click-lock SFX at 3s]

brief.title: One-Shot Wonder: The Golden Arrow Finisher
brief.hook_3s: Drawstring creaks, golden arrow ignites blue-and-gold sparks — release for one impossible, screen-shaking bullseye.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Tight close-up of the female archer drawing her bow on a duel platform — golden energy crackles around the arrowhead, aim trajectory line snaps into place across the screen.
3-7s: Slow-mo release. The arrow rockets down the trajectory arc, splitting through three target rings and a stack of barrels with satisfying chained impacts.
7-11s: Arrow strikes the rival duelist — their HP bar empties in one shockwave hit, KO badge slams in, coins and a legendary bow chest burst from the impact.
11-14s: Quick gear-upgrade montage: legendary bow tier-up animation with green +stat numbers popping, rarity backgrounds glowing yellow.
14-15s: Cut to game logo over the duel arena with CTA button pulsing.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Archery Clash!'s bright-blue arena banners (#2A9DF4) with vibrant yellow trajectory arcs and rarity glows (#FFD700), and green upgrade/stat pops (#32CD32). 3D stylized characters with slightly cartoonish proportions but realistic rim-lighting on the bow and arrow. Energy effects should feel premium-mobile, not anime — chunky particle bursts, screen-shake on impact, and slow-mo at the release frame to sell the "satisfying one-shot" beat. Keep the in-game HUD (health bars, turn indicator, gear slots) visible at all times so the ad reads as authentic gameplay.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Archery Clash! rotation is dominated by silent playables and a self-aware "I paid for this ad" UGC video — they have ZERO satisfaction/finisher-animation creatives in market. This brief borrows the trending charge→release→spectacular-finisher beat from the archetype WITHOUT the Dragon Ball IP, translating it natively to bow-and-arrow mechanics where it actually communicates the core loop. UA team: run this against young-male PvP audiences as a video-to-playable handoff; the one-shot KO fantasy is a proven CTR driver this account isn't currently exploiting.

(confident female whisper) / punchy upbeat trap at 95 BPM with rising tension riser / bowstring creak at 0s, energy crackle at 2s, deep sub-drop on final word.]
